title: PowerAttention: Exponentially Scaling of Receptive Fields for Effective Sparse Attention

method.name: PowerAttention
method.core_concept: Exponential scaling of receptive fields for effective sparse attention
key_components: Information flow analysis across six layers with 128-block example
Quantitative evaluation on Qwen2-7B with 32K context length
Model receptive field concept defined as maximum set of context tokens utilized during output generation
Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) model for analyzing information flow
comparisons: Compared to sliding window's linear expansion
Addresses limitations of existing static sparse attention designs
advantages: Significantly higher accuracy compared to sliding window's linear expansion
Better information retrieval capability that closely matches theoretical receptive field growth

context.problem: Quadratic complexity of attention mechanisms in Transformer-based LLMs when processing long contexts
context.solution_approach: Sparse attention to reduce computational complexity by restricting each token to attend to only a fixed number of tokens
method_categories: Static pattern: Pre-defined attention mask like sliding window attention
Dynamic pattern: Model trained with full attention and updates attention mask at inference stage

limitations_addressed: Information from tokens at certain positions cannot be retrieved by the final output
Low efficiency in expanding the receptive field layer by layer
